draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent client/project access on the left, with flexible card-based detail views on the right to handle variable information density
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (sidebar vs top tabs) combined with content container shape (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick context-switching between clients and hierarchical information organization
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers flat, sequential workflows with uniform content containers or top-level navigation

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to a voice that feels like a helpful friend rather than corporate software, reducing friction in daily CRM interactions
facade.label: Casual and warm
facade.acceptImplies: Build with conversational copy, reassuring microcopy, emoji or warm illustrations; prioritize approachability over formality
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward polished, buttoned-up language; minimize personality; emphasize credibility and process clarity over relatability

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent client/project access on the left, with flexible card-based detail views on the right to handle variable information density
evidence.implication: User values quick context-switching between clients and hierarchical information organization

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to CRM design that feels personal and uncluttered rather than corporate and feature-heavy
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool; organic vs digital
facade.label: Warm, minimal interface
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize a soft color palette, generous whitespace, and human-centered typography over sleek dark modes or dense dashboards
facade.rejectImplies: The audience may prefer efficiency-first design with cooler tones, compact layouts, and more digital/technical aesthetic—suggesting a different mood axis worth exploring

facade.hypothesis: Freelance CRM should feel approachable and less corporate, using curved edges and gentle shapes to reduce intimidation and create a welcoming interface
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: The builder values a friendly, non-intimidating aesthetic that feels more like a tool for independent creatives than enterprise software
facade.rejectImplies: The builder may prefer a more professional, structured look with cleaner lines and defined geometry to signal seriousness and precision

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers want to scan client details without loading new screens—sliding panels over the main list keeps context visible and reduces cognitive load.
facade.axisTargeted: Fluid transitions vs. discrete navigation—whether the UI prioritizes smooth motion and layered revelation or instant, separate views
facade.label: List slides open
facade.acceptImplies: The design favors overlays, side panels, and gesture-driven reveals. Transitions matter. The CRM feels like one continuous space.
facade.rejectImplies: The design favors full-page navigation, modals, and discrete states. Speed matters more than fluidity. Each action is a clear step.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer viewing clients as individual cards (name, status, last contact) over dense database rows, because it feels more human and scannable.
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data (table/list view) vs. Human-scale cards (card view)
facade.label: Client cards instead of rows
facade.acceptImplies: Card-based layouts resonate; design the CRM around visual blocks, each client as a distinct entity. Prioritize whitespace and one-client-at-a-glance info.
facade.rejectImplies: Compact table/spreadsheet view is preferred; freelancers want to see many clients at once and cross-reference columns quickly. Optimize for density and multi-column sorting.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants all tools visible at once vs. hidden behind menus/modals—reveals preference for dashboard density and navigation style in a tool-heavy CRM
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator voice
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals desired emotional tone while managing client work
Whether user prefers data-dense tables/lists vs. card-based summaries with key info highlighted—reveals how they want to scan and process client/project information
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals preference for snappy responsiveness vs. polished, deliberate interactions in a fast-paced freelance workflow

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants all tools visible at once vs. hidden behind menus/modals—reveals preference for dashboard density and navigation style in a tool-heavy CRM
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals desired emotional tone while managing client work
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-dense tables/lists vs. card-based summaries with key info highlighted—reveals how they want to scan and process client/project information
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals preference for snappy responsiveness vs. polished, deliberate interactions in a fast-paced freelance workflow
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers managing multiple concurrent projects need tab-based organization at the top level rather than sidebar nesting, allowing faster project switching and reducing cognitive load from nested hierarchies
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (sidebar hierarchy vs. tab-based switching)
facade.label: Tabbed projects view
facade.acceptImplies: User values flat, parallel project access over deeply nested client-project relationships; likely manages many active projects simultaneously with frequent context switches
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers hierarchical organization where projects are subordinate to clients; suggests deeper relationship context matters more than raw project velocity

draft.summary: A lightweight CRM dashboard for freelancers to track projects, clients, and invoices in one place.
